sequences.name: Departing Passenger
sequences.description: Landside-to-airside progression: curbside → check-in/ticketing → security screening → concourse → gate → boarding. Core terminal processor sequence per FAA AC 150/5360-13A.

sequences.name: Arriving Passenger
sequences.description: Airside-to-landside: gate → concourse → baggage claim → curbside. Bypasses security in reverse direction. Post-security areas are sterile.

sequences.name: Outbound Baggage
sequences.description: Check-in counter → outbound baggage handling → aircraft. Runs parallel to departing passenger flow below the concourse level.

sequences.name: Inbound Baggage
sequences.description: Aircraft → baggage handling → claim devices → passenger pickup. Converges with arriving passenger at baggage claim hall.

sequences.name: Concessions / Amenities
sequences.description: Post-security retail and amenity loop off the concourse. Passengers browse concessions while moving toward gates. Not a mandatory sequence — overlays on departing passenger flow.

sequences.name: Passenger Embarkation
sequences.description: Linear progression: arrival → check-in → security → hold → board. No backflow. Passengers must not reverse direction or re-enter completed stages.

sequences.name: VIP / Loyalty Embarkation
sequences.description: VIP branch after security: post-security → VIP lounge → boarding bridge. Rejoins main flow at boarding.

sequences.name: Passenger Debarkation
sequences.description: Compressed high-throughput: ramp → FIS → baggage claim → meeter-greeter exit. No mixing with embarkation flow.

sequences.name: Baggage – Embarkation
sequences.description: Parallel system: baggage moves independently of passengers from laydown to vessel via BHS. Never a sequential bottleneck with passenger flow.

sequences.name: Baggage – Debarkation
sequences.description: Vessel → BHS equipment room → debark laydown → baggage claim hall. Converges with passenger debark at claim hall.

sequences.name: Staff / Operations Circulation
sequences.description: Back-of-house staff circulation connecting operations, CBP offices, break areas, and loading dock. Must not cross passenger flows.

sequences.name: Event Attendee Arrival
sequences.description: Public event flow: reception/registration → open event floor → premier hall or private suite. Visitors may move freely between event spaces after registration.

sequences.name: Private Event Suite Path
sequences.description: Branch for private event suite guests after registration.

sequences.name: Restaurant / Café Visitor
sequences.description: Public dining: reception → restaurant/café. May combine with event attendance or be standalone destination.

sequences.name: Community Program Visitor
sequences.description: Community wellness, recreation, and cultural programs on both levels. Registration → wellness/recreation/exhibit → meditation.

sequences.name: Event Load-In / Setup
sequences.description: Back-of-house logistics: load-in dock → staging area → vendor overflow → event floor. Must not cross public visitor paths during events.

sequences.name: Event Staff Circulation
sequences.description: Staff circulation connecting management, production, crew break, and service areas. Separated from public flows.
